description_long: This sculpture features an organic, tree-like form with a textured, bark-like surface and a branching structure. The main trunk rises vertically from a roughly circular base, splitting into two thick, upward-reaching limbs that curve gently at the top. One of the limbs is slightly shorter and more irregular, with a smaller offshoot extending outward. The surface coloration transitions from earthy browns at the base to a muted greenish-gray at the top, suggesting weathering or patina. The overall appearance is abstract yet reminiscent of natural wood, with a hollowed section visible in the upper portion. The base appears solid and stable, anchoring the sculpture firmly to the ground.
description: An abstract, tree-like sculpture with a textured, bark-like surface and two upward-curving limbs, colored in earthy browns and greenish-gray.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main trunk splitting into two limbs, with a hollowed section and a prominent offshoot on the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the curvature of the limbs and the protruding offshoot extending outward from the left limb.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the symmetrical split of the two main limbs and the textured surface running up the trunk.
description_view_3: Opposite side view reveals the back of the trunk and the offshoot extending to the right, with the limbs curving away.
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background.
